articles.title: Microsoft Fixes One-Click GitHub Dev Attack That Let Attackers Steal OAuth Tokens
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a one-click attack via Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) that makes it possible to steal a user's GitHub token. articles.title: Autonomous AI Tool Finds 2-Year-Old RCE Flaw in Redis (CVE-2026-23479)
articles.summary: Redis has patched a use-after-free in its blocking-client code that lets an authenticated user run arbitrary OS commands on the machine hosting the database. articles.title: Google DoubleClick Abused in New Malspam Campaign to Deliver DesckVB RAT
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malspam campaign that makes use of Google's DoubleClick domain as a way to evade detection and ultimately deliver a remote access trojan (RAT) named DesckVB RAT. articles.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Let Any App Steal Account Tokens via Leftover Debug Flag
articles.summary: A development flag left switched on in production builds of several Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled the check that limits account-token sharing to trusted Microsoft apps. articles.title: What 345 Days of Untested Exposure Looks Like at a Bank
articles.summary: A two-week penetration test can leave roughly 345 days of real-world exposure unvalidated. Sprocket Security explores why continuous testing is becoming critical as attack surfaces constantly change. [...]

articles.title: Hackers Target Global Stock Exchange in Espionage Operation
articles.summary: The attackers had access to a senior executive’s email account for 150 days and exfiltrated data for months. The post Hackers Target Global Stock Exchange in Espionage Operation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: The default HTTP/2 configuration of major web servers is vulnerable to an attack chain combining a compression bomb and a Slowloris-style hold. The post ‘HTTP/2 Bomb’ Exploit Knocks Web Servers Offline in Seconds appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Russia claims foreign spy agencies hacked officials' phones
articles.summary: In a statement, Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB) said it had uncovered what it described as a "large-scale operation" involving malicious software installed on the mobile devices of senior Russian officials.

articles.title: Red Hat removes tainted packages after software pipeline compromise

articles.summary: Police described the incident as a large-scale disclosure of sensitive personal information that posed a threat to both the affected individuals and the institutions they serve. The data was allegedly posted on multiple internet platforms.
